news.title: Trump eases pressure on Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh as inflation tops 4%
news.summary: President Trump's economic advisors are giving Warsh space, as the new Fed chairman, on interest rates. Warsh's comments come as the president repeats calls to cut.

news.title: Ex-Apollo CEO Leon Black says Jeffrey Epstein duped him out of more than $60 million
news.summary: "I knew Jekyll. I didn't know Hyde," Leon Black says about Jeffrey Epstein. "I knew him as a person, not as a pedophile," he says.

news.title: Ex-Trump advisor John Bolton pleads guilty to retaining national defense information
news.summary: John Bolton is one of several foes of President Donald Trump who has been indicted since Trump regained the White House. Bolton is a former U.S. ambassador to the United Nations.

news.title: Jeremy Grantham says this is 'the most expensive market in 'American history'
news.summary: Jeremy Grantham says soaring valuations around AI makes the U.S. stock market historically expensive. He says it's the first time the market has been in such a state since the 1930s.

news.title: OpenAI and Anthropic face new AI reality as companies shift from tokenmaxxing to efficiency
news.summary: Companies are tightening their AI budgets to focus on getting a return on their investment. That could dampen growth rates at OpenAI and Anthropic.

news.title: This imminent SpaceX development could make the stock even more volatile
news.summary: SpaceX is set to be added to the Russell 1000 after Friday’s close. It could join other major indexes in the days ahead.

news.title: I want to leave everything to my sons, but I’m terrified they’ll give it to my ex-husband. How do I prevent this?
news.summary: “I would not like my money to fall into the hands of my former spouse,” he says. “I’m not a cheater. I’ve never been a cheaser.”

news.title: Exuberance over earnings is becoming a problem for the stock market, according to this Wall Street veteran
news.summary: Jim Paulsen: Optimism about corporate profits often comes before a fall, says Jim Paulsen. Paulsen on CNN's "Larry King Live" Sunday at 9 p.m. ET.

news.title: I had gallbladder surgery. After I got home, the hospital asked me for a financial donation. Is this ethical?
news.summary: “The letter asked whether I had a favorite caregiver and if I would like to make a contribution in their honor,” he says. “The letters asked whether he had afavorite caregiver.”

news.title: El Niño’s coming back — and it could cost the global economy trillions
news.summary: An El Niño could disrupt agriculture, infrastructure and economywide productivity. Economists at the investment bank wrote that an El Niño can disrupt agriculture and infrastructure.
